title: Rose geranium face balm
subtitle: An anhydrous balm for dry skin with rose geranium essential oil
excerpt: A water-free face balm made from beeswax, shea butter, and jojoba oil, scented with rose geranium essential oil at 3% of the base weight. Because it contains no water, it needs no preservative and keeps for up to 12 months. Apply a rice-grain amount to dry areas.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): rose geranium safe use rates for facial products. Formulators Kitchen basic anhydrous balm ratios (formulatorskitchen.com, accessed May 2026).

content.text: (water-free) tradition. Beeswax sets the structure. Shea butter adds slip and softness. Jojoba is the liquid carrier, with a fatty acid profile close to the skin's own sebum. Rose geranium essential oil gives a floral, rosy scent. It is one of the kinder florals for facial use. A rice-grain amount goes on dry areas. A wax-based balm goes a long way.

before applying to the full face. Rose geranium essential oil has a first-trimester pregnancy caution; leave out the essential oil or substitute with calendula-infused jojoba oil if making during early pregnancy. Add the essential oil once the wax blend has cooled below 40 degrees Celsius to preserve the volatile compounds.

items.name: Beeswax pellets
items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade yellow or white beeswax pellets. Sets the balm firm enough to hold its shape in a tin.

items.name: Shea butter
items.prepNote: Unrefined shea butter for a soft, slip-rich texture.

items.name: Jojoba oil
items.prepNote: Golden or clear jojoba oil. Very stable; won't go rancid quickly.

items.name: Rose geranium essential oil
items.prepNote: 3% of base weight. Pelargonium roseum. Add once the blend cools to below 40 degrees Celsius.

content.text: Weigh the beeswax pellets, shea butter, and jojoba oil into the heatproof metal bowl. Set the bowl over a pan of simmering water, making sure the base of the bowl does not touch the water. Stir occasionally until fully melted and combined. Remove from the heat.

Allow the blend to cool to below 40 degrees Celsius before adding the rose geranium essential oil. Stir in the essential oil, then pour quickly into the salve tins before the wax begins to set. Work fast once the balm starts to thicken.
Leave the tins undisturbed at room temperature for at least 1 hour to set fully. Do not move them while the wax is solidifying, or the surface will wrinkle. Label with the making date and contents.

Store in a cool, dark place with the lid on between uses. The balm is stable for 12 months from making. Use clean dry fingers or a spatula to scoop it out; introducing water or bacteria will reduce the shelf life. Discard if the balm develops a waxy rancid smell or changes colour markedly.

recipe.yieldDescription: 2 x 50 ml salve tins (~98 g total)
